bench1_cognitive_shift.description: ~3k-token predictable prefix then abrupt pivot to complex reasoning

bench2_throughput_vs_context.description: Generation throughput at different context window sizes
bench2_throughput_vs_context.context_scaling_note: 14% throughput drop from 2048 to 8192 — linear with attention KV growth

bench3_power_reasoning.description: Sustained nuanced reasoning (debug loops, riddles) under tegrastats monitoring

efficiency_finding.verdict: MAXN_SUPER is both faster and slightly more energy-efficient per token — the GPU is more fully utilised, making each watt more productive
efficiency_finding.crossover_note: At 1B scale with Q4_K_M, the GPU is likely underutilised at 15W; MAXN removes the clock/power cap and lets it operate in a more efficient region of the power-perf curve

notes: 45-47 tok/s generation — well above real-time interactive threshold
Prefill 1.68s on 3k tokens — responsive for embedded applications
Context scaling: 47.0 → 44.4 → 40.6 tok/s (2k→4k→8k), ~14% drop over 4x context
17.3W peak: above 15W TDP — verify thermal headroom for sustained MAXN_SUPER use
MAXN_SUPER is the right mode when throughput and latency matter; 15W for battery/thermal budgets
